hawkish_phrases: growing confidence in the gradual convergence of inflation towards the Governing Council's aim
increasingly robust and broad-based economic expansion
upside risks to activity associated with the strong cyclical momentum
robust growth momentum, very favourable financial market and borrowing conditions, dissipated deflation risks
some further assessment of the CSPP's impact on market functioning and pricing should be carried out
dovish_phrases: measures of underlying inflation had yet to show more convincing signs of a sustained upward trend
convergence of inflation towards the Governing Council's aim remained conditional on a substantial degree of monetary policy accommodation
ample degree of monetary stimulus was still needed
extended period of time after the end of the net asset purchases
summary: The Governing Council decided to reduce monthly asset purchases from €60 billion to €30 billion starting January 2018, extending the programme for nine months until September 2018 or beyond if necessary, maintaining all policy rates unchanged and forward guidance on rates, reflecting growing confidence in the economic expansion but acknowledging that inflation dynamics remained subdued and required substantial continued accommodation.
